title: Star Saber XC-01 starfighter

{{Quote|Listen, I can handle a Star Saber. Just push the stick a little early…and use the Force! I can fly it!|Dace Diath|<ref name="DL" />}}
The '''Star Saber XC-01 starfighter''' was an experimental [[Starfighter/Legends|fighter]] produced by [[Republic Fleet Systems]], and tested by [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] during the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. Long, sleek craft, the single-seat starfighters nonetheless were equipped with [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrives]]. Although the [[Starship/Legends|ships]] were capable of swift acceleration, their low maneuverability-to-speed ratio and slow control stick reaction gained them a reputation as dangerous and unwieldy craft. Heavy [[Blaster cannon/Legends|blaster cannons]] and [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]] gave the Star Saber more offensive and defensive capabilities than [[S-100 Stinger-class starfighter|contemporary fighters]], however, and the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] pressed the craft into service despite their design flaws. From their initial [[Battle of Empress Teta|combat mission]] against the [[Krath/Legends|Krath]] stronghold of [[Cinnagar/Legends|Cinnagar]] until the retirement of their line with the advent of the [[Aurek-class tactical strikefighter|''Aurek''-class tactical strikefighter]], the Star Saber was largely flown by Jedi, who could compensate by using [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] for the fighter's design flaws, which were never corrected.

==Description==
{{Quote|The ''stick'' was givin' me a sluggish response!|Dace Diath|<ref name="DL" />}}
At 13 meters in length, the [[Republic Fleet Systems]] Star Saber XC-01 [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] was long, low, and sleek. Protruding from the narrow hull were two wings fitted with weapons outriggers; two fins emerged from the tail of the craft, above the two engines. A military [[Snubfighter/Legends|snubfighter]] available to civilians only through regulated sales, the Star Saber was equipped with [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]] and two heavy [[Blaster cannon/Legends|blaster cannons]]—weapons that were significant improvements over those of the fighter's contemporaries. A single-seat craft, with 45 kilograms of cargo capacity,<ref name="KOTORCG" /><ref name="L" /> the fighter nonetheless was equipped with a [[Hyperdrive/Legends|Class 2.0 hyperdrive]] and a week's worth of consumables. While the Star Saber was quite fast at [[Sublight engine/Legends|sublight]] speeds, capable of attaining 1,100 [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] per hour in [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]],<ref name="PotJ" /><ref name="S" /> and accelerating to such speeds in only seconds, the craft was ungainly and not nearly as maneuverable as other fighters of its period—[[Krath/Legends|Krath]] [[CX-133 Chaos fighter]]s could outmaneuver them with ease. However, due to the needs of the time, the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] pressed the fighters into service, despite knowing their flaws.<ref name="KOTORCG" />

==Role==
The Star Saber XC-01, from its development during the [[Krath Holy Crusade]], was considered an inefficient but necessary tool to combat the threat posed by the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]].<ref name="PotJ" /> Considered an experimental vessel during its testing phase on [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]] in [[3997 BBY]], the Star Saber was nonetheless pressed into service against Krath forces,<ref name="DL" /> which it was designed to combat.<ref name="PotJ" /> In an [[Battle of Empress Teta|assault]] on the Krath throneworld of [[Empress Teta/Legends|Empress Teta]], [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Dace Diath]] used the Star Saber in both [[Starfighter combat/Legends|aerial dogfighting]] and surface assaults.<ref name="DL" /> Although the production of the fighters was almost halted after initial test runs,<ref name="KOTORCG" /> and the design of the fighter was never perfected due to its inefficiency against Chaos fighters,<ref name="PotJ" /> the Star Saber was nevertheless brought into full-scale production and authorized for limited sale to civilians in order to recoup losses associated with its design. [[Pilot/Legends|Pilots]] willing to utilize the Star Saber during its two-decade production run included [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], who were capable of compensating for the craft's shortcomings by using their abilities with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. However, once Republic Fleet Systems developed the [[Aurek-class tactical strikefighter|''Aurek''-class tactical strikefighter]] shortly before [[3976 BBY]], the Star Saber design was abandoned.<ref name="KOTORCG" />

==History==
{{Quote|Uh-oh—This ship is even ''more'' sluggish than the one I ''crashed''.|Dace Diath|<ref name="DL" />}}
In 3997 BBY, the Republic faced a crisis in the form of the Krath, a Sith cult from the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Empress Teta system]]. After taking control of the [[Star system/Legends|system]] by means of a [[Political uprising of the Empress Teta system|coup]], the Krath turned their attention on the larger [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]. During their attacks on Republic [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]], swarms of Krath Chaos fighters were willing to sacrifice themselves by crashing into enemy vessels. The Star Saber XC-01 was developed by Republic Fleet Systems to answer the threat posed by such attacks,<ref name="PotJ" /> creating a craft capable of attaining high velocity in seconds, with more advanced weaponry than its contemporaries.<ref name="KOTORCG" />

In their experimental phase, Star Sabers were tested on the [[Great Jedi Library/Legends|Jedi library]] world of Ossus, by Jedi pilots such as Dace Diath and [[Cay Qel-Droma]]. Both Jedi pursued [[Dragon-bird robot drone|Robotic drones]] created to simulate the flight of the highly evasive [[dragon-bird]]s of [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]] during the tests, with each pilot attempting to destroy the targets at high atmospheric speeds. It was in these tests that the Jedi pilots discovered a major design flaw: the Star Sabers were not nearly as maneuverable as other fighters of the time, and had slow reaction to control stick manipulation. Although he managed to score at least one kill against the drones in his fighter, Diath crashed into a mountainside, discovering that the Star Saber was not proportionately maneuverable for its speed. Although Diath survived the crash through his skill with the Force, the flaw was exposed to all potential pilots of the new craft.<ref name="DL" />

[[File:Starsaber.jpg|thumb|left|250px|A Star Saber XC-01 starfighter being tested on Ossus.]]
Nevertheless, for lack of other available craft on Ossus for an upcoming Jedi mission to extract the undercover Jedi Knight [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] from the Krath throneworld, the Star Saber XC-01 was pressed into immediate service. Diath himself volunteered to fly the vessel, reassuring his companions that with some adjustment and the guidance of the Force, he'd be able to handle the craft. Diath traveled to Empress Teta in his Star Saber, along with Jedi pilots [[Oss Wilum]], [[Tott Doneeta]], and [[Shoaneb Culu]] in other fighters. The entire group escorted an [[Onderon warbeast transport|Onderonian transport]] containing the assault force of Jedi and [[Beast Riders]] from Onderon on their [[drexl]] war mounts. As the raid on the [[Iron Citadel/Legends|Krath citadel]] took place, Diath and the others flew cover for the strike team. Although the mission was an overall failure, with Qel-Droma choosing to join the Krath and become [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], Diath managed to take out a Krath anti-air emplacement in his Star Saber.<ref name="DL" />

Despite its obvious design flaw, the Star Saber XC-01 was nonetheless mass-produced by Republic Fleet Systems and saw common usage during the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]] that ultimately encompassed the Krath Holy Crusade and saw the Republic at war with the forces of the Sith under the command of Ulic Qel-Droma and another Jedi-turned-Sith, [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]].<ref name="KOTORCG" /> Although the Chaos fighters used by the Krath were capable of flying circles around Star Sabers, the Star Sabers' flaws were never corrected.<ref name="PotJ" /> The need for more firepower simply outweighed the desire to work out the kinks in the ships' design. In the war and the era that followed, the pilots who chose to fly the fighters included Jedi who could compensate for their shortcomings with the Force. The Galactic Republic eventually made the Star Saber available for civilian purchase in order to recoup losses associated with their development, and the craft continued to be manufactured until the release of the Aurek tactical strikefighter much later.<ref name="KOTORCG" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Star Saber XC-01 first appeared in the issue [[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 6|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 6]], part of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith|''Dark Lords of the Sith'']] story arc of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Tales of the Jedi'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comics]]. The comic depicts the Star Saber as an experimental starfighter undergoing testing by the Jedi, who find it lacking, only to use it in later battles out of necessity. ''Tales of the Jedi'' penciller [[Art Wetherell]] originally drew the craft in the issue. The Star Saber XC-01 is also featured in the [[Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith (audio drama)|''Dark Lords of the Sith audio drama'']], where it is flown by Jedi [[Tott Doneeta]] instead of Dace Diath.

The Star Saber received more history and statistics from [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' in their [[2002]] supplement ''[[Power of the Jedi Sourcebook]]'' and [[2008]] ''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'', the latter of which included an updated image of the craft.

===Inconsistencies===
A number of statistical discrepancies exist between the Star Saber's depictions in ''Power of the Jedi Sourcebook'' and ''Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide''. While ''Power of the Jedi'' describes the fighter as being 6.75 meters in length, with 25 kilograms of cargo space, the newer ''KotOR CG'' provides figures of 13 meters and 45 kilograms. The latter statistics more accurately reflect the Star Saber as depicted in ''Tales of the Jedi'', and are used in this article for that purpose. Furthermore, ''Power of the Jedi'' gives the craft as having a Class 1.5 hyperdrive, while ''KotOR CG'' denotes Class 2. As well, ''KotOR CG'' gives the Star Saber a faster atmospheric speed of 1,280 kilometers per hour than ''Power of the Jedi''{{'s}} 1,100 km/h. For consistency, the updated statistics from the ''KotOR CG'' are provided.

==Appearances==
